docstring:
/**
 * @brief Global static initializer for the C++ iostreams library in this translation unit.
 *
 * Compiler-generated constructor (named `_GLOBAL__sub_I_main` because `main` is
 * the first function with static storage duration in this TU) that runs before
 * `main`. It constructs the translation-unit-local `std::ios_base::Init` sentinel
 * object `_ZStL8__ioinit`, which guarantees that the standard streams
 * (`std::cin`, `std::cout`, `std::cerr`, `std::clog` and their wide
 * counterparts) are initialized before any use.
 *
 * It then registers the matching `std::ios_base::Init::~Init` destructor with
 * `__cxa_atexit`, passing `&_ZStL8__ioinit` as the object pointer and
 * `&__dso_handle` so the destructor is invoked at program termination (or when
 * the shared object containing it is unloaded).
 *
 * @note This function takes no parameters and returns no value. It is emitted
 *       automatically by the compiler whenever a translation unit includes
 *       `<iostream>` (or any header that pulls in the `std::ios_base::Init`
 *       trick), and is invoked by the C++ runtime via the `.init_array` /
 *       `.ctors` section — it is not intended to be called directly.
 * @note The reference count maintained inside `std::ios_base::Init` ensures
 *       that the standard streams are constructed only once even if multiple
 *       translation units emit their own copy of this initializer.
 */
assumptions: Identified the function as the standard libstdc++ static initializer for std::ios_base::Init (the canonical _GLOBAL__sub_I_main emitted when a TU includes <iostream>).
Mapped the indirect call at offset 0x12d0 on &_ZStL8__ioinit to the std::ios_base::Init constructor based on the standard pattern.
Mapped the tail-call to L_1230 with (Init dtor, &_ZStL8__ioinit, &__dso_handle) arguments to __cxa_atexit registering the destructor.
Dropped the stack-canary load/compare residue (var0+0x3ff8 / fs:0x28) — it is a compiler-generated stack protector artefact with no source-level meaning.
Dropped the bare `nop`, push/pop rbp prologue/epilogue scaffolding.
divergences: [low] other: The offset 0x12d0 call is assumed to be the Init constructor; this is the canonical pattern for this symbol and is conventionally correct.
[low] dropped_side_effect: Stack canary load was dropped, but it's a compiler artifact with no source-level semantics.
